creator: scheidell@FreeBSD.org
text:
Frank: attachment one is the diff from sed (.bak). I put it in as a sed so that:
A) it won't break upstream fix if they fix it (but you can remove if they did)
B) eliminates a tempoary files/patch-* for when they do.

Your choice as maintainer:
Send this upstream (please do)

your choice:
1) wait for upstream to fix
2) approve this patch (attachment #2), it combines this pr and pr ports/168963


The problem is mail/smtp-cli:

according to:
<http://search.cpan.org/dist/IO-Socket-SSL/SSL.pm>

and
<http://cpanforum.com/posts/13713>

The problem is with ASSP. They use the SSL_version parameter of IO::Socket::SSL in a way which was never documented. It only 
worked because it ignored wrong values until version 1.70, but with 1.70 additional functionality was added to SSL_version 
so that it now needs to check the syntax and rejects bad values.
You might fix the issue for you if you delete any references to SSL_version from assp/assp.pl

and:

grep SSL_version *
smtp-cli:        SSL_version => 'SSLv3 TLSv1',


this use of SSL_version is invalid.

see from
http://search.cpan.org/dist/IO-Socket-SSL/SSL.pm:


SSL_version

    Sets the version of the SSL protocol used to transmit data. The default is 'SSLv23', which auto-negotiates between SSLv2 
and SSLv3. You may specify 'SSLv2', 'SSLv3', or 'TLSv1' (case-insensitive) if you do not want this behavior.

    You can limit to set of supported protocols by adding !version separated by ':'. The default is 'SSLv23:!SSLv2' which 
means, that SSLv2, SSLv3 and TLSv1 are supported for initial protocol handshakes, but SSLv2 will not be accepted, leaving 
only SSLv3 and TLSv1.


-- 
Michael Scheidell, CTO
SECNAP Network Security Corporation
http://people.freebsd.org/~scheidell
